memory_items.title: OpenPyXL Cell Access Pattern
memory_items.lesson: openpyxl Worksheet objects do not have .range() method; iterate cells explicitly using .cell(row=..., column=...)
memory_items.generalization: Always verify openpyxl API methods match the object type; use explicit cell iteration for bulk operations on ranges

memory_items.title: Dimension Consistency Validation
memory_items.lesson: Input data columns must match expected product/item count for proper index alignment in multi-dimensional calculations
memory_items.generalization: Validate that source data dimensions match target computation grid size before writing loop-based operations

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Structure Exploration

memory_items.generalization: Gather structural metadata first to inform range definitions and iteration bounds

memory_items.title: Error-Driven Iterative Correction
memory_items.lesson: Use traceback information to pinpoint exact API mismatches or index out-of-bounds issues
memory_items.generalization: Treat errors as diagnostic signals; adjust loop bounds and method calls based on specific exception types

memory_items.generalization: Implement verification print statements for edge cases (diagonals, boundaries) to validate output integrity

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### OpenPyXL API Compatibility
- **Worksheet Objects:** Do not support `.range()` like some libraries; iterate cells using explicit `.cell(row=..., column=...)` calls.
- **Bulk Operations:** Use nested loops over row/column indices rather than expecting range-select methods.

### Dimension Consistency Checks
- Validate source column count matches expected item/product count before building lookup arrays.
- Ensure output grid rows and columns correspond to input data dimensions to prevent index mismatches.

### Pre-Implementation Reconnaissance
- Print sheet names, inspect header rows, and verify data ranges before executing transformations.
- Gather structural metadata (max_row, max_col, non-null counts) to inform safe iteration bounds.

### Error-Based Debugging Workflow
- Leverage traceback exceptions to identify API incompatibilities or off-by-one indexing errors.
- Adjust loop ranges and data array lengths immediately after encountering IndexError or AttributeError.

### Post-Write Verification
- Always print sample cell values from critical positions (diagonal cells, corners, mid-grid) to confirm calculations executed.
- Log transformation statistics (rows processed, customers found, output path) for auditability.
